codeReview.summary: New test file tests/devin-agent.test.ts (142 lines) adds 17 well-structured unit tests across 4 describe blocks covering all feature requirements: config field correctness, detection logic (both branches), non-universal classification, and uniqueness/no-duplicate guards. The test uses vi.hoisted() + vi.mock('fs') to control existsSync — the correct Vitest pattern for mocking detection logic. Code is clean, properly formatted (format:check passed), and follows existing project test patterns (mirrors tests/xdg-config-paths.test.ts structure closely). The remaining diff is mission state bookkeeping (.mission/ files) which is correctly maintained. No production code was modified, as expected for a test-only feature.

issues.description: configHome is derived using `process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME || join(home, '.config')` (falsy-coalescing) while src/agents.ts uses `xdgConfig ?? join(home, '.config')` (nullish-coalescing via xdg-basedir). If XDG_CONFIG_HOME were set to an empty string, the test and source would disagree. In practice this is a non-issue since XDG_CONFIG_HOME is never empty, and the existing xdg-config-paths.test.ts uses the same pattern.

summary: Pass. 17 comprehensive unit tests for Devin agent config covering all 4 expected behaviors (config fields, detection branches, non-universal classification, validate-agents). Tests follow existing project patterns, use correct Vitest mocking, and the commit includes no production code changes. One non-blocking observation about configHome derivation style difference vs source.
